Sun Qiang… What Was He Like?

	Date: 04/2008
	Replies: 10
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Sun Qi was from Fuchun county, Wu commandery. He was the grandson of Sun Fu through a son who served as General who Exerts Might (奮威將軍).

At the age of sixteen, he was nominated as Flourishing Talent and got to met Sun Quan. He was noted by Zhou Zhao for having good looks and a handsome appearance.

In 249, he was married to Fan Ji, daughter of Fan Shen.

After the heir to the Wu empire, Sun Deng, died in 241, Sun Quan made Sun He the heir. However, Sun Ba also had a good claim to being the heir, and factions formed in favor of either two. Sun Qi belonged to the Sun Ba faction. He constantly pandered to the prince and slandered his enemies, including Sun He. Sun Quan was becoming increasingly irritated with the factional turmoil and in 250, he forced Sun Ba to commit suicide and deposed Sun He. In the aftermath, Sun Qi was executed. Fan Ji would refuse to remarry after his death.

full_text:
Sun Qi (Zhongrong) 孫奇 (仲容)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Sun Qi (孫奇), styledZhongrong (仲容), was born in Fuchun county, Wu commandery. Executed for being associated with Sun Ba. Sun Qi was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. Sun Qi lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

After the heir to the Wu empire, Sun Deng, died in 241, Sun Quan made Sun He the heir. However, Sun Ba also had a good claim to being the heir, and factions formed in favor of either two.
